title: Prosaptia vomaensis
taxonomy: Prosaptia vomaensis is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a member of the genus Prosaptia, which consists of various species of ferns. The classification of Prosaptia vomaensis places it within the division Pteridophyta, which comprises all ferns and their allies. Further classification includes the class Polypodiopsida and the order Polypodiales. The exact classification of Prosaptia vomaensis is subject to ongoing research and refinement.

intro: Prosaptia vomaensis is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, commonly found in Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to alleviate fever, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Bioactive constituents of Prosaptia vomaensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Prosaptia vomaensis is often prepared as a tea or infused in water, although it is also used to make ointments and topical creams for wound care and skin conditions.
